signals.reasoning: BTC is improving, but macro is still RED and the cleaner edges are still in selective alt setups rather than the major itself. Neutral remains the disciplined baseline.
ETH is participating in the rebound, but not with enough relative strength to justify upgrading it over the stronger alt expressions. Best left neutral.
SOL has bounced well, but the price/OI pattern still reads more like short-covering than a clean durable breakout. Not strong enough to promote.
CHILLGUY is still constructive, but it is already extended and no longer has a clean squeeze edge. Better as a watchlist runner than a fresh long.
VVV is rallying, but the move still looks more like short-covering than a stable fresh trend entry. Not enough edge for a new recommendation.

WLD remains the cleanest actual long on the board. Price and open interest are still rising together, the broader structure stays upward, and it continues to show real relative strength rather than a one-off squeeze spike.
WCT has a squeeze element and trend-supported price action, but the execution quality is poor and the spread is too wide to trust as a clean fresh trade. Watch it, do not force it.
VIRTUAL is recovering, but the setup is still more ordinary rebound than standout opportunity. Not weak enough to short and not strong enough to buy over the better names.
AIXBT is still too thin and too middling to offer a meaningful edge. No reason to manufacture a trade here.
STABLE still has the strongest raw squeeze-long profile on the sheet. Funding remains strongly negative while price stays firm, so it keeps a reduced-size long upgrade even with macro still weak.
GOAT remains damaged, but the downside is already too mature and unstable to trust as a clean fresh short. Weak structure alone is not enough.
